macro_segments.title: Review of Previous Discussion: Terumah Repayment Based on Size vs. Value

micro_segments.title: Summary of Yesterday's Debate on Terumah Payment Requirements
Rav Pappa's Theory: Abba Shaul Requires Both Size and Value

macro_segments.title: Rav Pappa's Retraction and the Proof from Biblical Verse

micro_segments.title: Introduction to Rav Pappa's Retraction (V'Af Rav Pappa Hadar Bei)
The Me'ilah Offering and the Intentional vs. Unintentional Divide
The Logical Question: Why Need a Verse if We Can Infer from Other Mitzvot?
Distinguishing Punishments: Karet vs. Mita B'Yedei Shamayim
The Core Distinction: Minimum Size (Kezayit) vs. Smaller Amounts
Resolution: The Verse is Necessary to Teach About Korbanot
Rabba and Rav Sheshet Challenges to the Explanation

macro_segments.title: Rebbi's Position on Intentional Me'ilah and the Question of Minimum Size

micro_segments.title: Who Holds That Intentional Me'ilah Results in Death? Rebbi's View
Rebbi's Derivation: Learning Me'ilah from Terumah (Gal'al Chet Chet)
The Problem: If Me'ilah is Derived from Terumah, Both Should Require Kezayit
Rav Pappa's Challenge: How Do We Know Rebbi Holds Like the Rabbanan?
The Question of Abba Shaul: Does Rebbi Hold Like Him Regarding Value?
The Inference: Rav Pappa Must Have Retracted His Original Theory

macro_segments.title: Return to the Baraita and Further Explanations of the Kal VaChomer

micro_segments.title: The Intent vs. Non-Intent Distinction in Other Mitzvot vs. Me'ilah
Examples: Shabbat Harvesting Without Intent; Me'ilah Warmth Without Intent
Alternative Formulation: Mis'asek (Unintended Action) in Other Mitzvot
Me'ilah's Stringency: Liability Even Without Intention
Rav Chama bar Yitzchak's Explanation Using Intent/Non-Intent

macro_segments.title: The Question of Terumah from Chametz on Pesach

micro_segments.title: Returning to the Earlier Question: Can You Separate Terumah from Chametz?
The Deriving Source: Titen Lo (Give to Him) and Not for Fire
The Distinction: Terumah Chametz Cannot Be Used for Benefit
The Matvei of Rav Huna: Terumah from Tamei Cannot Be Separated
The Case of Terumah Teme'ah: Works B'Dieved Because It Had She'at Kosher
Chametz's Problem: Never Had She'at Kosher (Fermented While Still Attached)
Rav Huna's Disagreement: Chametz Doesn't Work Even If It Became Chametz After Harvesting
The Word Reishit: Implying That Separating Terumah Should Permit the Remainder
Yatzah Zo: With Chametz, the Remainder Doesn't Become Permitted

macro_segments.title: The Ruling of Rav Acha bar Avya: Tamei Grapes Produce Tahor Wine for Nesachim

micro_segments.title: The Amazing Ruling: Tamei Met Can Press Grapes and Produce Tahor Wine
Condition: Must Be Less Than an Egg's Volume (Pachot Mi-Kebeitzah)
The Mechanism: Juice Considered Separate from Grapes, Like Water in a Cup
Why Juice Doesn't Become Tamei: Requires a Minimum Size (Kebeitzah) to Become Tamei
Rav Chisda's Objection: Disregarding Rabbi Yochanan's Ruling
The Counter-Challenge: If Juice Is Separate, Why Can't We Use Tamei Berries?
The Response: Grapes Were Never Hukhshar (Prepared) for Tumah
The Complication: Food Needs Preparation (Hukshra) Through One of the Seven Liquids
Rava's Response: We're Concerned About Takala (Dangerous Precedent)
Abaye's Counter: We Allow Using Tamei Terumah Oil as Fuel, So Why Worry About Takala?
The Solution: We Prevent Takala by Making the Item Disgusting (Kli Ma'us)

topical_tags.term: Rav Pappa reverses opinion on Abba Shaul
Karet (kareit) vs. Mita B'Yedei Shamayim (death from heaven)
Rebbi vs. Rabbanan regarding Me'ilah punishment
Abba Shaul: value-based (Pruta) vs. size-based liability
Juice considered separate from fruit (Mifkad Pikdi)
Hukshra (preparation of food for tumah through liquids)
Rav Chisda's rejection of Rabbi Yochanan's ruling
Kli Ma'us (disgusting vessel) to prevent Takala
